commonMistakes: Mutating props or state instead of producing the next value.
Adding state or an Effect for a value that can be derived during render.
Using global state before identifying the real owner and consumers.
Optimizing renders without a measured user-facing problem.

contentAuthority.canonical: New JS2Next Release 1 curriculum and independently authored content.

choices.feedback: Engineering judgment balances clarity and cost. The concept should serve the requirement rather than become an end in itself.
choices.label: Use Reusability and Advanced React Patterns when it makes intent, ownership, or data flow clearer; avoid adding it mechanically when a simpler, equally explicit solution is sufficient.

questions.explanation: Engineering judgment balances clarity and cost. The concept should serve the requirement rather than become an end in itself.

sections.body: Reuse may come from functions, custom Hooks, composition, configurable components, or domain abstractions.
sections.title: Reusability strategies

sections.body: Controlled, compound, and render-prop patterns distribute responsibility differently between provider and consumer.
sections.title: Composition patterns

sections.body: Render props pass behavior through a function, HOCs wrap components, and compound components coordinate related children through shared context.
sections.title: Candidates for render props, HOCs, and compound components

sections.explanation: A compound component coordinates related children through context.

summary: Reusability and Advanced React Patterns connects Reusability strategies, Composition patterns, Candidates for render props, HOCs, and compound components.
Use the topic to make behavior and decisions explicit rather than memorizing isolated syntax.
Verify understanding by explaining a new example and answering the topic quiz without looking back.
title: Reusability and Advanced React Patterns

topicQuiz.failurePolicy: Show explanations, add missed objectives to local review, and allow immediate or later retry.

whyThisMatters: Learners frequently use reusability and advanced react patterns without a clear model of why it behaves as it does; this lesson makes that model explicit. It belongs to the **Professional React Workflows and Ecosystem** module.
